publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-21 23:14:25 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Personal papers and clippings from years in office as a member of the House of Representatives. Papers include files relating to campaigns and elections, constituent requests, general files, files relating to bills, invitations, files relating to military and social security cases, military academies, veterans, gun control, the Vietnam War and the draft, aging and senior citizens, and files relating to local issues such as post offices, local airports, floods, interns, federally-funded projects, and Cornell University.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Howard W. Robison practiced law in Owego, N.Y., served as county attorney of Tioga County, N.Y. from 1946 until 1956 when he was elected as a Republican to the Eighty-fifth Congress to fill the vacancy caused by the resignation of W. Sterling Cole; he was reelected and served in the House of Representatives until his retirement in 1974.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on use:
userestrict.p: Case files are available to legitimate scholars with the understanding that the identity of individuals in these files is not to be published or otherwise released in such form that any individual mentioned in the records can be identified. Reference can be made to individual cases, but only on an anonymous basis.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Howard W. Robison papers, #3394. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, invitations, other personal papers, bills, press releases, and clippings from years in office as a member of the House of Representatives. Papers include files relating to campaigns and elections, constituent requests, general files, files relating to bills, invitations, files relating to military and social security cases, military academies, veterans, gun control, the Vietnam War and the draft, aging and senior citizens, and files relating to local issues such as post offices, local airports, floods, interns, federally-funded projects, and Cornell University. Also, tapes of "Robison Reports," 1968-1974, interviews on national events, John T. Calkins, interviewer. Copies of newsletters, Your Washington Report and Robison Reports, sent to constituents, 1958-1971.

controlaccess.subject: Politicians -- United States.
Airports -- New York (State)
Vietnam War, 1961-1975 -- United States.
Draft -- United States.
Postal service -- United States -- Postmasters.
Firearms -- United States -- Law and legislation.
Older people -- United States -- Services for.
Social security -- United States.
Floods -- New York (State)
Military pensions.
Veterans -- Medical care.
Elections -- New York (State)
Politics, Practical.

p.extref: A copy of the printed guide is available by clicking here. This covers a majority of the collection, but there are some additinal boxes not listed. The guide to the Robinson Reports tape recordings section starts on page 142. (2 MB Searchable PDF)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To Improve the financial management of Federal assistance programs, to facilitate the consolidation of such programs, to strengthen further congressional review of Federal grants-in-aid, to provide a catalog of Federal Assistance programs & to extend and amend the law relating to intergovernmental cooperation.

did.unittitle: H.R. 850 - To extend to all unmarried individuals the full tax benefits of income splitting now enjoyed by married individuals filing joint returns.
H.R. 1460 - To provide that the fiscal year shall coincide with the calendar year.
H.R. 2571 - To provide for a limitation on budget expenditures & net lending for fiscal year not to exceed $229.2 Billion
H.R. 3119 - To authorize the Secretary of Interior to establish the Lincoln Home National Historic Site in the State of Illinois & for other purposes.
H.R. 3612 - To amend the Internal Revenue Code to allow credit against income tax for certain higher educational expenses.
H.R. 3787 - To fix date of citizenship of Alfred Lorman for purposes of War Claims Act
H.R. 3863 - To create a catalog of Federal assistance Programs, and for other purposes.
H.R. 4716 - To establish an Office of Consumer Affairs in the Executive Office of the President and a Consumer Protection Agency.
H.R. 4932 - To repeal the Connally Hot Oil Act.
H.R. 4935 - To terminate the Oil Import Control Program.

H.R. 5412 - To make available to certain organized tribes, bands or groups of Indians residing on Indian reservations established under State Law certain benefits, care or assistance for which federally recognized Indian tribes qualify as recipients.
H.R. 5413 - To amend the Water Resources Research Act

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To increase the authorization for water resources research and institutes, and for other purposes.

did.unittitle: H.R. 5243 - To amend title 18 USC to prohibit the establishment of emergency detention camps and to provide that no citizen of the United States shall be committed for detention or imprisonment in any facility of the United States Government except in conformity with the provisions of title 18.
H.R. 5089 - To set standards of ethics and financial disclosure in campaigns for election to Federal Office.
H.R. 5092 - To provide certain amounts of television program time for candidates for Federal offices during general elections.
H.R. 5098 - Tax Credits for Political Contributions.
H.R. 5959 - To amend the Federal Water Pollution Control Act.
H.R. 5963 - To amend Section 8 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act, as amended, and for other purposes.
H.R. 5967 - To amend the Federal Water Pollution Act, as amended.
H.R. 5971 - To establish an Environmental Financing Authority to assist in the financing or waste treatment facilities and other purposes.
H.R. 6363 - To amend the Economic Opportunity Act

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To authorize a legal services program by establishing a National Legal Services Corporation, and for other purposes.

did.unittitle: H.R. 6492 - To provide Federal assistance for special projects to demonstrate the effectiveness of programs to provide emergency care for heart attack victims by trained persons in specially equipped ambulances.
H.R. 6842 - To authorize a White House Conference on Education.
H.R. 6884 - To prohibit discharge of any military material or other refuse into navigable waters of the United States or into international waters, and for other purposes.
H.R. 19329 - To require the Department of Defense to determine disposal dates and methods for disposing of certain military material.
H.R. 7414 - To amend the State Technical Services Act

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To make municipal governments eligible for technical services under the Act, to extend the Act though fiscal year 1974, and for other purposes.

did.unittitle: H.R. 7750 - To provide a comprehensive child development program in the Department of Health, Education & Welfare.
H.R. 7903 - To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act, and for other purposes.
H.R. 8028 - To incorporate the Gold Star Wives of America.

H.R. 8222 - To provide that expenses incurred in the construction of the Elmira Psychiatric Center in the city of Elmira, New York, shall be eligible as local grants-in-aid for purposes of title I of the Housing Act
H.R. 8387 - To provide a procedure for the exercise of congressional and executive powers over the use of any Armed Forces of the United States in military hostilities, and for other purposes.
H.R. 9089 - To amend the Railway Labor Act.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To provide more effective means for protecting the public interest in national emergency disputes involving the railroad and airline transportation industries, and for other purposes.

did.unittitle: H.R. 9256 - To authorize the Secretary of the interior to establish the Lincoln Home National Historic Site in the State of Illinois, and for other purposes.
H.R. 10801 - To provide for the elimination, over a ten year period, of the mandatory oil import control program.
H.R. 11098 - To amend the federal Voting Assistance Act.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To enlarge the class of persons provided with the opportunity to vote in Federal, State and local elections by absentee ballot.

did.unittitle: H.R. 12660 - To amend the Internal Revenue Code.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To allow a deduction for contributions to candidates for elective Federal Office or to political parties.

did.unittitle: H.R. 13078 - For the relief of Herman Frederick Erben.
H.R. 14831 - To amend the Internal Revenue Code.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To allow a credit against income tax to individuals for certain expenses incurred in providing higher education.

did.unittitle: H.R. 15957 - To amend the Water Resources Act.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To increases the authorization for water resources research and institutes, and for other purposes.

did.unittitle: H.R. 16027 - To amend the Federal Water Pollution Control Act as amended.
H.R. 16029 - To amend the Federal Water Pollution Control Act, as amended, to provide financial assistance for the construction of waste treatment facilities and for other purposes.
H.R. 16030 - To establish an environmental financing authority to assist in the financing of waste treatment facilities and for other purposes.

H.R. 17278 - To make available to certain organized tribes, bands, or groups of Indians residing on Indian reservations established under State law certain benefits, care, or assistance for which federally recognized Indian tribes qualify as recipients.
H.R. 18615 - Voluntary Military Manpower Procurement Act.
H.R. 18876 - To provide for fiscal responsibility through the establishment of a limitation on budget expenditures and net lending (budget outlays) for the fiscal year, and for other purposes.
H.J. Res. 433 - H.Con. Res. 698 - Robison Legislation
Co-sponsorship of Leg. (before get our copy of bill)
